reasoning:
The target to beat is ~233.8 protests per 30 days (the average over the 360 days preceding the Jun 8, 2025 forecast date). 

1. Res 1 (2025-06-15): The window runs May 16 - Jun 14. Almost 80% of this window is in the past. It perfectly captures a massive ongoing protest wave against the new "Security Decree" (which passed the Senate on June 4), as well as broad strikes and pro-Palestine demonstrations. Because nationwide protests generate dozens of individual ACLED events across different cities, this wave will heavily spike the 30-day count well past the annual average.

2. Res 2 (2025-07-08): The window runs Jun 8 - Jul 7. Backlash momentum from the Jun 4 Security Decree will likely carry strong protest action into June. However, as July approaches, Italian institutional function begins to slow for summer, lowering the volume. I expect this to lean above average, but with less certainty.

3. Res 3 (2025-09-06): The window runs Aug 7 - Sep 5. This precisely overlaps with the Italian 'ferragosto' summer period. Cities empty out, parliament goes on recess, and major strikes are legally restricted or suspended in transit sectors. Historically, this is predictably the lowest point for protests in the year, so it is very likely to fall below the 233.8 average.

4. Res 4 (2025-12-05): The window runs Nov 5 - Dec 4. Autumn is traditionally peak protest season in Italy for students and labor unions (e.g., large student clashes in Nov 2024). Baseline models strongly suggest November outperforms the annual average. However, the 6-month forecast horizon introduces high noise.

In accordance with instructions to increase uncertainty for distant horizons, the probabilities have been explicitly pulled closer to 0.5 as the dates get further out.
